title: Storyline video editing
appliesWhen: user wants to edit selected videos into a short film
user asks to compose multiple video clips by story order
user asks to reorder selected videos by plot, shot sequence, or narrative logic
用户想把多个视频按剧情剪成短剧、成片、故事视频或完整片段
用户要求根据故事情节重排选中的视频素材
requiredInputs: at least two selected video nodes

workflow.goal: Read the currently selected video nodes before planning.
workflow.output: Selected video node ids and safe node metadata.

workflow.goal: Verify each selected video can be resolved by node id and read duration, size, fps, and availability.
workflow.inputRule: Pass only selected video node ids. Do not hand-write file paths.

workflow.goal: Convert selected video nodes into storyline clips using node names, prompts, metadata, connected reference nodes, canvas position, and probe metadata.
workflow.output: One storyline clip for each selected video node.

workflow.goal: Create narrative groups, orderedIds, and a timeline with reasons for every clip.
workflow.output: A structured timeline. orderedIds must include every input clip exactly once.

workflow.goal: Compose the final video strictly in planned storyline order.
workflow.inputRule: Use ids from storyline.planVideoOrder.orderedIds and set order to provided. Include the timeline in the compose request.

planningRules: Always call canvas.getSelectedNodes before any storyline video edit.
Always call media.probeVideo before describing or composing clips.
Do not guess, invent, or hand-write media file paths. Media tools must resolve paths internally from node ids.
Every selected video node must become exactly one clip in storyline.describeClips; this first version does not split one video into multiple clips.
Always call storyline.planVideoOrder before composing, unless the user explicitly asks to concatenate by selection order.
Before composing, show the user a concise storyline order summary with a reason for each clip.
For normal storyline editing, call media.composeVideos with order: provided and ids equal to storyline.planVideoOrder.orderedIds.
Do not use media.composeVideos order: selection for storyline editing unless the user explicitly says to use the selected order.
If media.probeVideo returns any failed video, stop and explain the structured error instead of composing.
If the planning confidence is low, explain the fallback basis such as shotIndex, story signals, scene, canvas position, or input order.
media.renderTimeline is not part of this first version.
